title: Coelogyne lumakuensis
taxonomy: Coelogyne lumakuensis is classified as a species of orchid. This orchid belongs to the genus Coelogyne, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. The species is further classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The genus Coelogyne is part of the tribe Coelogyneae, which is a subtribe within the subfamily Epidendroideae. Coelogyne lumakuensis is considered to be an endemic species from the island of New Guinea.

intro: Coelogyne lumakuensis, also known as the Lumaku orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in the Pacific Island region, for its health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to boost the immune system and alleviate various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne lumakuensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems.
